final_plan.summary: Scaffold a new Angular single‐page application under projects/phoneword-converter that lets a user type a phone number and displays the possible letter combinations based on standard T9 mapping (2→abc, 3→def, …, 9→wxyz). Encapsulate mapping logic in a service, wire up a simple input/output component, and cover the logic with unit tests.

bootstrap_commands.purpose: Scaffold a fresh Angular application with default settings

target_files.details: Implement component logic to bind to the input, invoke PhoneMapperService, and store/display the result combinations.
Add an input field for the phone number, a button to trigger mapping, and a display area for the resulting word combinations.
Create a service that encapsulates the T9 digit-to-letter mapping and generates all possible letter combinations for a given digit string.
Register PhoneMapperService in the module's providers array (if not already providedIn: 'root').

constraints: Adhere to Angular style guide and company coding conventions (see ARCHITECTURE_AND_CODING_GUIDELINES.md).
Keep all logic client‐side; no backend or database integrations.
Use reactive forms for input validation.
validation: Application compiles and runs (`ng serve`) without errors.
Unit tests cover the service logic with ≥90% coverage.
Manual QA: entering digits 2–9 yields correct combinations; invalid characters are ignored or flagged.
clarification_summary: Requirement was straightforward; no further clarifications needed.
review_guidelines: Verify separation of concerns: component for UI, service for logic.
Ensure T9 mapping covers digits 2–9 and handles edge cases.
Check for adherence to Angular best practices and company style rules.
Confirm adequate unit tests and test coverage reports.
discovery_hints: Search for any existing Angular examples in the monorepo for conventions.
Check README.md for workspace-wide linting/test commands.

goals: Provide a SPA to convert a phone number into letter combinations using T9 mapping.
Encapsulate mapping logic in a reusable service.
Deliver a clean, responsive UI for input and output display.
Ensure high unit-test coverage for mapping logic.
acceptance_criteria: Entering a valid digit string (2–9) shows the correct letter combinations.
Non-digit or out-of-range characters are handled gracefully (ignored or validated).
Service mapping logic is fully covered by unit tests.
Project builds and runs with standard npm/ng commands.
non_goals: No backend API or database persistence.
No advanced predictive T9 or dictionary lookup features.

ambiguities: Whether to limit the number of combinations for long digit strings (e.g., pagination or max cap).
Desired UX for selecting or copying from multiple combinations.
